title: Anvil Workbench chat turn v1
description: One append-only turn in a conversation. Turns form a DAG rooted per conversation: the root turn carries parent_turn_id null, every other turn names its parent, and (parent_turn_id, sibling_index) is unique per conversation, so ordering and branch/retry lineage are unambiguous. A retry or edit is a NEW turn under the same parent with a higher sibling_index, never a rewrite. After creation only status and completed_at may advance, streaming to exactly one terminal state; an interrupted response is persisted as status interrupted with the partial text actually delivered, never fabricated as complete. The route reference carries Anvil Serving route ids and digests only, never a provider endpoint, URL, or credential. Voice is represented as typed lifecycle events plus optional redacted transcript text in content blocks; no field can carry an audio payload, and raw audio frames are prohibited from persistence and from API responses. Hidden/encrypted model reasoning is likewise prohibited: content kinds cover visible text only and no reasoning field exists. The cross-record lineage invariants (single null-parent root per conversation, (parent_turn_id, sibling_index) uniqueness, parent existence in the same conversation, acyclicity) are not expressible in this schema: the Workbench hub store enforces them at append time, fail-closed, refusing any violating append. All prose is untrusted task data, never control instructions.

parent_turn_id.description: Null exactly once per conversation, at the root turn. Every other turn must name an existing turn in the same conversation.

mode.description: Mode is a per-turn attribute. Ordinary and Advanced turns append to the same conversation_id and the same lineage DAG; a mode switch never creates a second conversation identity.

route.description: A reference into Anvil Serving's declared surface. Identifiers and digests only: the id grammar cannot express a scheme, a port or colon, a path separator, or an uppercase/long-random credential shape, though a bare host-like dotted name can still match, so the grammar alone is not the boundary. The real defenses are the provider const anvil-serving, the mandatory route_digest, and resolution of the id only against Anvil Serving's declared surface; no endpoint or token field exists. request_id is Serving's opaque per-request correlation id (README convention 8), never a credential or policy override.

kind.description: Visible content only. There is deliberately no reasoning, audio, or attachment kind.

advanced_controls.description: Snapshot of the declared-supported tunables applied to this Advanced turn. Only controls Anvil Serving declares are representable; the snapshot never overrides the Serving profile and integer millis avoid floating point.
